note: Archive/Sensitivity.lean:56,97 (namespace Sensitivity) - abbrev Q (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool with def adjacent {n} (p : Q n) : Set (Q n) := {q | ∃! i, p i ≠ q i} is exactly the hypercube graph (vertices = length-n bit strings, edges = differ in one coordinate), used ad hoc for Huang's sensitivity theorem rather than packaged as a general SimpleGraph instance. Verified full name Sensitivity.Q.adjacent against declaration-data.json oracle (fixed missing namespace prefix from an earlier draft).
verify_note: Decl confirmed to exist (Archive/Sensitivity.lean:56-57,97); docstrings literally read 'The hypercube in dimension n' (Q) and 'the adjacency relation defining the graph structure on Q n' (adjacent), with adjacent = differ-in-exactly-one-coordinate (Hamming distance 1) and a proven symmetry lemma (adjacent.symm) confirming undirected-graph behavior; Wikidata Q958394 label/description ('hypercube graph' / 'graphs formed by a hypercube's edges and vertices') matches; no other hypercube-graph formalization exists in Mathlib, so this ad hoc but mathematically exact vertex-type+edge-relation pair is a fair 'exact' call despite lacking a packaged SimpleGraph instance.

note: Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Measure/CharacteristicFunction/Basic.lean:246 - proves two finite measures on a complete second-countable inner-product space are equal if their (multivariate) characteristic functions agree everywhere; mathematically equivalent content to Cramér-Wold (a random vector's law is determined by all 1-D linear-projection laws, since charFun μ t is the 1-D charFun of the projection along t evaluated at 1) but stated via the joint char function directly, not phrased as 1-D projections.
verify_note: Decl confirmed to exist at Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Measure/CharacteristicFunction/Basic.lean:246 with signature/docstring matching the note verbatim (charFun μ = charFun ν → μ = ν for finite measures on a complete second-countable inner-product space); no dedicated Cramér-Wold-named decl exists anywhere in Mathlib (grep for Cramér/Wold turns up nothing; 'Cramer' hits are all Cramer's-rule false positives) and Wikibrain confirms Q966837 is currently not_formalized, so 'related' (not 'exact', since the projection-based phrasing isn't literally present) is an honest, non-overclaiming label.
